prompt:
A highly detailed full-page steampunk video streaming website interface themed around the Victorian electrical age, designed like an antique brass-and-iron machine panel with dark bronze metal textures, rivets, pipes, gears, ornate filigree, glowing Edison bulbs, and sepia-toned imagery. At the top left, place a modern white logo reading "Pollo" with a small pink-red rounded badge containing "AI", and directly below it the text "GPT Image 2" in white, overlaid on the vintage interface. Center the header with a large embossed brass title reading "ElectroVid" on a dark metal plaque, and a smaller engraved tagline underneath reading "THE FUTURE IN MOTION". Add a horizontal navigation bar with exactly 5 items: a home icon, "TRENDING", "INNOVATIONS", "DOCUMENTARIES", and "ENTERTAINMENT", plus a search field on the right containing the placeholder text "Search ElectroVid..." and a square search button. In the upper right corner, include a round analog gauge or clock built into the frame.

Use a three-column vintage web layout. In the left sidebar, create a panel titled "POPULAR CHANNELS" containing exactly 3 circular channel emblems stacked vertically, each with a name, subscriber count, and a "SUBSCRIBE" button: 1) "THE PATENT OFFICE" with "27.4K SUBSCRIBERS", 2) "THE ROYAL SOCIETY" with "18.7K SUBSCRIBERS", 3) "GENERAL ELECTRIC" with "35.1K SUBSCRIBERS". The emblems should look heraldic and industrial, with aged metallic borders.

In the main content area, feature a large video player showing a dramatic sepia debate scene between two late-19th-century inventors in suits, facing each other across a table with electrical apparatus, a glowing bulb, and lightning on a chalkboard behind them reading "AC vs. DC". The left figure points accusingly; the right figure gestures back. Keep both faces obscured by soft rectangular blur blocks. Add a video control bar with a play button, progress bar, volume icon, timecode "08:47 / 22:31", settings icon, and fullscreen icon. Beneath the player, place the large serif video title "The Truth About AC vs. DC: The Final Debate". Below that, show the channel row with a small circular avatar, the channel name "The Electrical Age", and metadata "142K views • 3 days ago". On the right side of this row, add a brass button reading "LIKE 5.8K".

Below the featured video, add a section header "LATEST VIDEOS" and exactly 3 video cards in one row. Card 1: a sepia thumbnail of a man holding up a glowing light bulb, face obscured by a blur block, duration label "10:12", title "My New Incandescent Bulb", creator "Thomas Edison", and metadata "89K views • 5 days ago". Card 2: a sepia thumbnail of a Wardenclyffe-style tower crackling with electricity, duration "15:36", title "The Wonders of the Wardenclyffe Tower", creator "Nikola Tesla", and metadata "120K views • 1 week ago". Card 3: a sepia thumbnail of an early automobile on a city street, duration "12:08", title "The Automobile Revolution", creator "Henry Ford", and metadata "110K views • 1 week ago". Under the cards, center a brass button reading "VIEW ALL VIDEOS".

At the bottom, add a metal footer plaque with the text "© 1893 ElectroVid, Ltd. All rights reserved." Surround the whole composition with decorative mechanical borders, exposed pipes, pressure gauges, cogs, corner ornaments, and warm amber lighting. The overall style should blend a YouTube-like video platform layout with a luxurious Victorian steampunk machine aesthetic, using dark brass, copper, blackened steel, parchment beige, and sepia photography, with crisp readable typography and a polished cinematic UI mockup look.
